Farn Porrrrcal PnecrrcEs CouirarssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
September 15,2004
Jeffrey R. Dodge
PACE International Union
Post OfEce Box 349
Marttnez, C A 9 45 53 -003 4
Re
Your Request for Adrrice
Our FiIe No. A-04-185
Dear Mr. Dodge:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of PACE International Union
for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the'.Acf).I
QUESTION
You are the political director for the PACE Intemational Union's local political
action committee and have asked whethei the method used by the organization to raise
firnds for its local political action committee complies with the law.
DISCUSSION
Your letter states that, as approved by a vote of your membership, $0.50 per
member per month of your regular monthly union dues is deposited in a segregated fund
for the purposes of funding your state and local political activities. Expenditures from
this fund are subject to the recorlmendation of your executive board and the approval of
your membership at a regular union meeting.
Labor unions and other organizations that receive contributions totaling $1,000 or
more in a calendar year qualifu as "committees" under the Act and must file periodic
reports disclosing their financial activities. (Section 82013.) The term "contribution"
includes any portion of dues paid by members of an organizattonthat are earmarked or
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91074. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
,@


FileNo. A-04-185
Page No. 2
used for the purpose of making contributions and expenditures to support or oppose state
and local candidates and ballot measures. (Section 82015; regulation 18215.)
The Act does not speciff orprohibit anyparticular fund-raising method. As long
as all receipts and expenditures are properly disclosed and the organization complies with
the Act's recordkeeping requirernents and the applicable contribution limits, the
fundraising method you describe would not violate the provisions of the Act. (Sections
84101, 85303.)
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (et6)
322-5660
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
